reasoning: The hypotheses `[CommGroup G] [IsSimpleGroup G]` faithfully represent "G is an abelian simple group". The conclusion `IsCyclic G ∧ ∃ G_fin : Fintype G, Nat.Prime (@card G G_fin)` is logically equivalent to "G ≅ Z_p for some prime p": forward, any Z/pZ is cyclic and of prime order; backward, any group of prime order is cyclic and isomorphic to Z/pZ. The `IsCyclic G` conjunct is technically redundant (implied by prime cardinality) but doesn't change the logical content. The "do not assume G is finite" caveat from the informal statement is correctly respected — no `[Fintype G]` appears among the hypotheses; finiteness is derived as part of the conclusion via `∃ G_fin : Fintype G`. Since `Fintype` is a subsingleton in Mathlib, the existential correctly captures "G is finite with prime cardinality". Thus the formalization is neither weaker nor stronger than the informal claim.
